hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_desc: Describe your typical work day and tell the time with at / on / in -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- A Day at Work
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 5 -- A Day at Work
lesson.grammar_point: telling the time and prepositions of time (at / on / in)

listenings.text: Hi Flavia, this is Paulo from the office. Here is the plan for tomorrow. We start at nine o'clock in the morning with a short meeting. At half past ten, you have an appointment with a new partner. Lunch is at one o'clock, and after lunch there is a workshop. Please arrive a few minutes early, because the room is small. We finish at five o'clock. Thank you, and have a good evening.

Good morning, everyone. This is a short reminder about our new office hours. From Monday, we open at eight o'clock in the morning and close at six o'clock in the evening. The lunch break is from twelve to one. On Friday, we finish early, at four o'clock. Please check your appointments and start your meetings on time. Remember, the workshop is on Wednesday, in the afternoon. Thank you, and have a great week.

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia arrives at the office at eight o'clock in the morning. Then she starts her day with emails and a short meeting. At half past twelve, she has lunch with a colleague. In the afternoon, she has an appointment with a new partner. She finishes at six o'clock and goes home in the evening.
